title: S1 mechanical economy workers catalog
purpose: B1.2 defines s1 mechanical economy workers catalog for the agent-driven expert system. Cognition & routing — [S0](B1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md)–S4, conductor, workers, dual-stack.
scope: Owns `B1.2` only; siblings under `B1` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §4 — Branch B — Cognition & routing plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#4-branch-b-cognition-routing-plane).
behavior_steps: When route-tier classifies a turn as S1 and `spawn_workers` is true, the conductor spawns economy-tier implement workers from `model-policy.json` with task-card scope and `allowed_reads` caps—never genius models for mechanical bounded edits.

S1 workers execute catalog lookups, shell verification, and bulk search under orchestrate-subagents contracts and must not expand scope, dual-write journal/state, or mark tasks complete without evidence paths.
The conductor selects worker roles using Plane E catalog component refs so recurring implement patterns map to verifier or tool-operator roles instead of improvising role choice each wake.
[B1.1](B1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md) scripts remain mandatory before S1 workers run when file-derived routing, validation, or verify already exists in `scripts/`.

Mis-routed S3 architecture work assigned to S1, or S1 tasks escalated to genius without `model_escalation`, stops at H2 until route-tier re-classifies capability and dual-writes corrected `model_tier`.

narrative: B1.2 defines s1 mechanical economy workers catalog for the agent-driven expert system. Cognition & routing — [S0](B1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md)–S4, conductor, workers, dual-stack. In practice, the system when route-tier classifies a turn as s1 and `spawn_workers` is true, the conductor spawns economy-tier implement workers from `model-policy.json` with task-card scope and `allowed_reads` caps—never genius models for mechanical bounded edits, then s1 workers execute catalog lookups, shell verification, and bulk search under orchestrate-subagents contracts and must not expand scope, dual-write journal/state, or mark tasks complete without evidence paths.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-b 2026-06-28 -->

S1 covers mechanical work that still needs a model but not genius-tier judgment: bounded code edits from task cards, catalog lookups, shell verification, and bulk search. Economy workers from `model-policy.json` execute S1 under conductor contracts with `allowed_reads` caps.

S1 is the default implement tier when `spawn_workers` is true. Workers must not expand scope, dual-write journal/state, or skip evidence. The catalog in Plane E tells the conductor which worker role fits which task pattern. Mis-routing S3 architecture questions to S1 causes rework; mis-routing S1 mechanical tasks to genius tier wastes cost.
